ocr.text: In the realm of healthcare services and medical technology, understanding the surgical procedure mix is vital for optimizing
resource allocation and improving patient outcomes. This report presents a comprehensive analysis of the surgical procedures
performed by Fresenius SE, focusing on key metrics such as the number of procedures completed and the associated financial
implications. The data table delineates the distribution of surgical interventions, specifically highlighting areas such as Cardiac

Surgery and Orthopedic Surgery, which represent significant components of our operational portfolio.

In the past fiscal year, the
organization successfully completed 1,200 cardiac surgeries, generating a substantial revenue of CAD 15 million. This
translates to an impressive average revenue of CAD 12,500 per procedure, underscoring the high value associated with cardiac
interventions.

The high demand for these critical surgeries reflects the growing prevalence of cardiovascular diseases in the North American
population, necessitating ongoing investment in advanced surgical technologies and skilled personnel to ensure optimal patient
care. Moreover, the completion rate of these procedures stands at 5%, indicating a robust operational capacity to meet patient

needs efficiently.

In contrast, the report also encompasses data on orthopedic surgeries, with a total of 1,800 procedures
performed during the same period. This segment showcases the diversification of our surgical offerings, allowing us to cater to
a wider demographic while maintaining a focus on quality care. The combination of these surgical specialties not only enhances
our service portfolio but also contributes to our overall financial health. As such, monitoring these metrics will be crucial for

future strategic planning and resource management.

As we navigate the complexities of the healthcare landscape, the insights
derived from the surgical procedure mix data will inform our decision-making processes. By analyzing trends and performance
indicators, we aim to identify areas for improvement, ensuring that we remain at the forefront of medical innovation and patient
satisfaction. The successful execution of these surgical procedures is indicative of our commitment to excellence in healthcare
delivery, reinforcing our position as a leader in the medical technology sector. As we move forward, continued emphasis on
data-driven strategies will be essential for sustaining our competitive advantage and meeting the evolving demands of our
patient population.
